mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of dual-decline triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below 94.0 signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization that constrains corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 93.5 and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by CPI combined with elevated federal funds rates above 2.0% signals monetary tightening that increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of dual-trigger instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts above 650,000 units annualized signals strong residential investment demand and forward-looking economic optimism that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in real GDP growth and retail sales signals robust aggregate demand and consumer purchasing power that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% annualized and RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with strong consumer activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of dual-trigger instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
